prompt: Soviet Constructivist graphic composition in the precise manner of Alexander Rodchenko and El Lissitzky, 1922–1925. Bold diagonal composition at approximately 45 degrees from upper-left to lower-right. Strictly limited palette: pure red, black, white, and three tones of grey only — no other colors. Completely flat color areas with no photographic shading or gradient within any element. The composition shows a powerful bold red geometric beam or arrow form entering from the upper-left corner at full intensity — thick, vivid, dominant. This signal-form passes sequentially through five bold vertical black rectangular planes arranged diagonally across the composition, each plane fully present and structurally bold. After passing through the first black plane, the red beam becomes slightly thinner and shifts toward dark grey-red. After the second plane, it is medium grey and significantly thinner. After the third plane, it is pale grey and barely thick enough to read. After the fourth plane, it is an almost invisible light grey thread. After the fifth plane at lower-right, it has effectively vanished — only the faintest grey trace remains, barely distinguishable from the white background. The black planes themselves remain bold and unchanged throughout — they are not diminishing, only the signal passing through them is. Strong figure-ground contrast. Asymmetric but dynamically balanced composition. No text. No organic forms. No photographic elements. Should be indistinguishable from an authentic 1920s Constructivist graphic work.
tradition_name: Soviet Constructivist graphic design, Rodchenko and El Lissitzky tradition
tradition_approach: Targeted Constructivism's bold diagonal compositions, strictly red-black-white-grey palette, flat color with no photographic shading, and dramatic scale contrast — using the tradition's directional force logic to show a gradient signal entering at full red intensity from upper-left and diminishing to near-invisibility through successive bold geometric planes, the planes themselves remaining structurally dominant while only the passing signal vanishes.
